channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: The U.S. will resume Ukraine military aid and intelligence, as Kyiv approves ceasefire
item.description: The United States said it will resume sending military aid and intelligence to Ukraine, as Ukraine agreed to a Trump administration proposal for a monthlong ceasefire.
item.dc:creator: Joanna Kakissis, Michele Kelemen, and Eleanor Beardsley, MPR News

item.title: Democratic Rep. Raúl Grijalva of Arizona, champion of environment and progressivism, dies at age 77
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Democratic Rep. Raúl M. Grijalva, a champion of environmental protections and progressive ideals who took on principled but often futile causes during a two-decade career in Congress, died Thursday. Grijalva, who was 77, had risen to chair the U.S.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN GROVES and LEAH ASKARINAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Barely: House GOP passes government funding bill without help from Democrats
item.description: House Republicans this week passed a government funding bill 217-213 without assistance from Democrats, losing only one vote from Rep. Thomas Massie, R-Ky.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Death penalty, guns topics of 2 bills introduced in Nevada Senate
item.description: If it seems like death penalty cases drag out in the courts already, new legislation introduced on Thursday would ensure that the process slows down even more.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Top diplomats from G7 countries meet in Canada as Trump threatens more tariffs on US allies
item.description: LA MALBAIE, Canada (AP) — Top diplomats from the Group of 7 industrialized democracies held a first day of talks in Canada on Thursday as U.S. President Donald Trump’s trade and foreign policies have thrown the bloc’s once solid unity into disarray.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rep. Raúl Grijalva dies at 77
item.description: Rep. Raúl Grijalva (D-Ariz.), a more than two-decade veteran of the House who rose to high levels of power in the Democratic Caucus, died on Thursday. He was 77.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Oversight Democrats launch review of crypto reserve 'poised to enrich' Trump
item.description: House Oversight Democrats are encouraging President Trump to abandon his plans for a crypto reserve, calling it a strategy to enrich himself at taxpayer expense.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: DC fears revenue cliff as Congress debates its financial future
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Washington, D.C., which has often had a tenuous peace with the federal government when Republicans controlled Congress and the White House, is now facing its most urgent threat since it was given the power of Home Rule during the Nixon administration.
item.dc:creator: GARY FIELDS and ASHRAF KHALIL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump to invoke wartime Alien Enemies Act of 1798 to fast-track deportations of illegal immigrants
item.description: President Donald Trump is expected to invoke a centuries-old wartime act to target illegal immigrants for deportation, according to media reports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hear the death threats Republican senator received about Trump
item.description: Sen. Thom Tillis, R-N.C., his staff and family have been the target of extensive harassment and death threats, a memo released by his office on Thursday afternoon revealed.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Democratic Rep. Raul Grijalva dead at 77
item.description: Rep. Raul Grijalva, D-Ariz., died of lung cancer-related issues on Thursday morning, his office announced in a statement.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump's pick for ambassador to Canada says it's a sovereign state. Trump wants it as a US state
item.description: President Donald Trump's choice to be U.S. ambassador to Canada on Thursday said that America’s northern neighbor is a sovereign state, showing some daylight with Trump on the issue. The president has insisted that Canada would be better off as the United States' newest member. Former Rep.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Politics Friday: Minnesota’s paid leave law remains a flashpoint as launch nears
item.description: Friday at noon, MPR News politics editor Brian Bakst talks with two Minnesota lawmakers about state’s new paid leave program — scheduled to start in January of next year — and the legislation that could possibly delay or change eligibility.
item.dc:creator: Brian Bakst and Matthew Alvarez, MPR News

item.title: Direct aid to Israel should be phased out to 'reduce US leverage,' influential conservative groups argue
item.description: The Heritage Foundation is responding to backlash against a report that suggested the U.S. should phase out direct aid to Israel so that a future president could not cut it off at will.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Hitchhiker's Guide to where we stand with a potential government shutdown
item.description: Democrats are torn on how to vote for Trump's budget bill amid a looming government shutdown if an agreement is not made.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Trump administration demands UN agencies disclose any 'anti-American' ties
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — The Trump administration is demanding U.N. humanitarian agencies that receive or disburse U.S. funding fill out a questionnaire disclosing any ties to communism, socialism or anti-American beliefs, according to U.S. and U.N.
item.dc:creator: FARNOUSH AMIRI, MATTHEW LEE and ELLEN KNICKMEYER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: In lawsuit settlement, Texas Tech’s med school promises it won’t consider race in admissions
item.description: George Stewart sued Texas Tech University Health Science Center and five other medical schools claiming they rejected him in favor of lesser qualified students of color.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Jessica Priest, KPRC 2

item.title: Minnesota House bill to encourage sharing of immigration data with ICE introduced
item.description: The bill aims to prevent state and local governments from limiting their employees’ ability to share immigration data, including the legal status of immigrants, with federal authorities.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Thamer, MPR News

item.title: Canadians face stiffer rules for extended US stays
item.description: Canadians who stay in the U.S. for 30 days or longer face fines and charges for not registering as foreign nationals.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: Wall Street ends manic day modestly lower after briefly dropping more than 10% below its record
item.description: The U.S. stock market fell further Tuesday following President Donald Trump’s latest escalation in his trade war, briefly pulling Wall Street 10% below its record set last month. And like it's been most of the past few weeks, the market’s slide on Tuesday was erratic and dizzying.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Florida bill would require some elementary school students to learn cursive
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. (WFLA) — A new Florida bill would require some elementary school students to learn cursive writing. The bill, HB 921, would require all public schools to provide cursive writing instruction to students in grades 2 through 5.
item.dc:creator: Katlyn Fernandez, WFLA, NBC

item.title: President Trump's tariffs raise concerns of economic recession
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – President Donald Trump’s tariffs on products from Canada and Mexico have some Americans worried that they will lead the economy into a recession.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, PIX 11

item.title: Renewed debate sparks discussion on 'My Safe Florida Home' program
item.description: Governor Ron DeSantis wants more Floridians to benefit from the "My Safe Florida Home" program, which provides storm-hardening home improvements for homeowners.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Former US solicitor general to submit written arguments in Eric Adams case
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A former U.S. solicitor general who was brought in to help a federal judge decide whether to accept a Justice Department request to drop corruption charges against New York City Mayor Eric Adams is set to submit written arguments Friday. Judge Dale E.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11
